findings.evidence: Line 185 says "The zero subcoalgebra is finite"; lines 186-187 give only `Module.Finite R ((⊥ : Subcoalgebra R C).toSubmodule)`.
findings.fix: Change the docstring to say the underlying submodule of the zero subcoalgebra is finitely generated.
findings.issue: `bot_isFinite` is documented as saying the zero subcoalgebra is finite, but the declaration proves `Module.Finite R ((⊥ : Subcoalgebra R C).toSubmodule)`, i.e. finite generation of the underlying submodule.

findings.evidence: Line 93 says "The subcoalgebra spanned by a group-like element is finite"; lines 94-95 state `Module.Finite R (groupLikeSpan ... g).toSubmodule`.
findings.fix: Document this as finite generation of `(groupLikeSpan g).toSubmodule`, not finiteness of the subcoalgebra itself.
findings.issue: `groupLikeSpan_isFinite` is documented as a finite subcoalgebra result, but the statement only gives `Module.Finite` for the underlying submodule of the singleton span.

findings.evidence: `rg -n "finite-subcoalgebra|Subcoalgebra\.IsFinite|Module\.Finite R .*toSubmodule"` finds only this docstring plus the two `Module.Finite ... toSubmodule` instances.
findings.fix: Replace the phrase with a concrete description, e.g. "the singleton span and its `Module.Finite` instance."
findings.issue: The module docstring says the singleton span and instance are used by "the finite-subcoalgebra API", but this PR does not define such an API; it only exposes `Module.Finite` instances for underlying submodules.

summary: The new module docs are mostly present, but the finite-generation instances are documented as finite subcoalgebras/API, which overstates the actual `Module.Finite` statements over a general `CommSemiring`.
